figures.caption: Table 1: Comparison of VOS datasets. ∗ indicates the segmentation subset.
Fig. 3: Category-instance distribution of the UW-VOS dataset. Due to space constraints, we only visualize the top 10 subcategories within each superclass.

Table 3: In-domain benchmark results of VOS methods trained and evaluated on UW-VOS. ∗ indicates that due to hardware constraints, a reduced training budget was employed; see the Appendices for details. Inference speed (FPS) and GPU memory usage (GiB) are measured on a single 3090 GPU. For SAM2, video frames are offloaded to CPU memory to balance inference speed and memory usage. Bold and underline denote the best and second-best results, respectively.

Table 7: Benchmark results of video object tracking methods on UW-VOS validation set. P and Pnorm: precision metrics measuring center location accuracy (raw and sizenormalized); AUC: area under the success plot curve; AO: average overlap.

Fig. 1: Example videos from the proposed UW-VOS dataset. The selected targets are highlighted by colored masks. The figure illustrates several representative characteristics in the UW-VOS dataset, including complex interactions among multiple targets (a), small objects within similar clusters (b, c), turbid water conditions (d), camouflaged targets (e), target exit and re-entry (f), complex lighting and shadows (g), as well as viewpoint changes (h).

Table 2: Cross-domain evaluation results on UW-VOS validation set. We report the mean J&F on DAVIS17 and YouTube-VOS19 as open-air reference; values in parentheses indicate the gap relative to UW-VOS J&F .

Fig. 11: Qualitative results on UW-VOS. We compare the performance of XMem [11], Cutie [9], and SAM2 [38] on six challenging cases. These cases include fine-grained structures (a), camouflage (b), target exit and re-entry (c), small objects (d), complex interactions (e), and crowded scenes (f).
Table 5: Attribute-based performance analysis on UW-VOS validation set. We

Table 6: Definitions of attributes in UW-VOS. We adopt a subset of attributes from WebUOT-1M [55] with further adjustments and extensions. The top part presents generic attributes for video object segmentation, while the bottom part highlights several challenging attributes specific to underwater scenarios.

Table 4: Ablation study of SAM-U on UW-VOS validation set. Inference speed (FPS) and GPU memory usage (GiB) are measured on a single 3090 GPU. Training time (hour) and memory usage (GiB) are determined by the experimental setup detailed in the appendix.
Fig. 9: (a) Overall framework of SAM-U. (b) Architecture of the Underwater Domain Adaptation (UDA) block.
